task_completion.description: Verify the target channel 'Unstable_Service' is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) Administrator dashboard or channels list shows a channel named 'Unstable_Service'.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Unstable_Service' is configured as a TCP Listener on port 7000
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Source settings for 'Unstable_Service' show the connector type as 'TCP Listener' and the port set to '7000'.

task_completion.description: Verify the watchdog channel 'System_Watchdog' is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NextGen Connect Administrator dashboard or channels list shows a channel named 'System_Watchdog'.

task_completion.description: Verify 'System_Watchdog' is configured as a JavaScript Reader with a 10-second interval
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Source settings for 'System_Watchdog' show the connector type as 'JavaScript Reader' and the polling frequency/interval set to 10000 ms (or 10 seconds).

task_completion.description: Verify the watchdog script uses ChannelUtil to monitor, restart, and log
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code in the 'System_Watchdog' reader source contains logic using 'ChannelUtil' to check the state of 'Unstable_Service', a command to start/restart it, and code to append the message 'Restarted Unstable_Service' to '/home/ga/watchdog.log'.

task_completion.description: Verify the watchdog successfully writes to the log file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor displaying the contents of '/home/ga/watchdog.log', showing timestamped entries containing the exact text 'Restarted Unstable_Service'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual NextGen Connect Administrator Java application being used to configure the channels, rather than just editing backend XML files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the watchdog script contains actual conditional logic
integrity.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code should show an IF statement checking the channel state (e.g., checking if it is NOT 'STARTED') before writing to the log, rather than a script that blindly writes to the log file every 10 seconds.

integrity.description: Verify the log file output appears genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The timestamps in '/home/ga/watchdog.log' should look realistic and sequential, not manually typed or copy-pasted identical strings in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target channel must be named 'Unstable_Service' and configured as a TCP Listener on port 7000. The watchdog channel must be named 'System_Watchdog' and configured as a JavaScript Reader with a polling interval of 10000 ms (10 seconds). The watchdog script must use the ChannelUtil API to monitor 'Unstable_Service', restart it if it is not in a STARTED state, and append 'Restarted Unstable_Service' to '/home/ga/watchdog.log'.
